summary: Install-time host reconnaissance beacon to attacker-controlled Burp Collaborator endpoint
details: On `npm install`, the package's `preinstall` hook executes `node index.js`, which collects host and user identity (hostname, platform, arch, home directory, username/uid/gid/shell, OS details, current working directory, and the output of `whoami` and `id`) and POSTs the data as JSON to a hardcoded out-of-band endpoint at `https://6cjy9tle5weq8pr6m8r5znzd349vxmlb.oastify.com/detox56` (Burp Collaborator). Package metadata is empty (no description, author, or repository) and the scoped name `@civitatis/bot-ui` is consistent with a dependency-confusion lure targeting a private internal package. Installer machines that resolve this public package leak identifying information to the attacker, enabling targeted follow-on attacks.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
